---
title: "Background"
---

This site presents [Journal Checker Tool](https://journalcheckertool.org/) (JCT)
data for the TU9 universities. The link between an agreement, its participating
institutions, and the journals it covers comes from a voluntary, partly manual
process, not an authoritative feed from publishers. This page explains how that
data is assembled and how this site matches it.

## How the data is assembled

1. A publisher and a library consortium (or a single institution) negotiate a
   [transformative agreement](https://esac-initiative.org/about/transformative-agreements/).
2. The consortium or institution registers it in the
   [ESAC Transformative Agreement Registry](https://esac-initiative.org/about/transformative-agreements/agreement-registry/).
   Sharing the terms is
   [voluntary](https://esac-initiative.org/about/transformative-agreements/share/),
   and agreements are registered at the consortium level rather than per member.
3. The JCT data team crawls ESAC regularly, then contacts each consortium for
   the covered titles and the institutions with access. This list is curated by
   hand, not scraped (Hope 2020).
4. The JCT publishes the result as CC0
   ["Transformative Agreements Public Data"](https://journalcheckertool.org/transformative-agreements/)
   with an [API](https://journalcheckertool.org/apidocs).
5. This site consumes that data and matches agreements to TU9 universities by
   [ROR](https://ror.org/) identifier.

A single agreement can give different institutions access to different title
sets. The JCT records this in its own per-agreement sheets. There is no fixed
one-to-one relationship between an agreement and its journal or institution
lists (Kiley 2023).

## How this site matches agreements

An agreement is listed for a university when one of the university's ROR
identifiers appears among the agreement's participating institutions. A
university is represented by its own ROR ids. This includes its library and
university hospital where these hold a separate ROR.
In the current data a university hospital is
only ever listed on agreements its university already holds. It adds no coverage
of its own; its ROR is recorded in the `members_ror` column to show which
agreements name the library and/or hospital.

## Caveats

Only agreements reviewed and confirmed Plan S–compliant (JCT 2026b) appear in the
public index. An expired agreement is dropped about three months after it ends
(JCT 2026a). Coverage depends on consortia choosing to register and to respond to
the JCT's requests. This is a curated snapshot, not a complete or historical
record.

Independent checks of this kind of JCT-based matching find it accurate
but incomplete — high precision, lower recall (de Jonge et al. 2025). A related
JCT-based analysis was likewise designed to underestimate rather than
overestimate the open access it counts (Jahn 2025).

## References

- Hope, H. (2020). Enabling accurate results within the Journal Checker Tool.
  *sOApbox, official blog of cOAlition S*, 18 November 2020.
  <https://www.coalition-s.org/blog/enabling-accurate-results-within-the-journal-checker-tool/>
- Kiley, R. (2023). Introducing new search features in the Journal Checker Tool
  for Transformative Agreements. *sOApbox, official blog of cOAlition S*,
  22 September 2023.
  <https://www.coalition-s.org/blog/introducing-new-search-features-in-the-journal-checker-tool-for-transformative-agreements/>
- de Jonge, H., Kramer, B., & Sondervan, J. (2025). Tracking transformative
  agreements through open metadata: Method and validation using Dutch Research
  Council NWO funded papers. *Quantitative Science Studies*, 6, 1215–1227.
  <https://doi.org/10.1162/qss.a.24>
- Jahn, N. (2025). How open are hybrid journals included in transformative
  agreements? *Quantitative Science Studies*, 6, 242–262.
  <https://doi.org/10.1162/qss_a_00348>
- Journal Checker Tool. (2026a). What happens to "expired" Transformative
  Agreements? *FAQ*.
  <https://journalcheckertool.org/faq/#what-happens-to-expired-transformative-agreements>
- Journal Checker Tool. (2026b). What is the Journal Checker Tool for? *FAQ*.
  <https://journalcheckertool.org/faq/#what-is-the-journal-checker-tool-for>
